programme.title: Carol
programme.desc: Carol Schlosberg is a talented American artist enjoying a vacation with her boyfriend in Puerto Escondido, Mexico. But when she goes for a solo walk on the beach, she never returns – and the hunt is on for a killer.

programme.title: Murder Camp!
programme.desc: In Northern California, an anonymous 911 caller tells Shasta County police there’s a body at Campsite 47. In a remote alpine wilderness they discover the battered body of 20-year-old Lora Sinner.

programme.title: Gerard, Sheila & Darin
programme.desc: In the sweltering heat of a New Orleans summer, a young Japanese tourist goes missing. Soon after, a badly decomposing body is discovered. Suspects are plentiful, but the men photographed on her digital camera are one step ahead of the police.

programme.title: Chris
programme.desc: A young successful businessman leaves it all behind to travel the world. But when he falls out of contact, his family soon fear the worst. With no evidence or suspects, can investigators solve the mystery behind his whereabouts before it’s too late?

programme.title: Maria & Marina
programme.desc: When two young women disappear on a dream backpacking trip through South America, only part of the mystery is solved when strange packages are found near a beach on Ecuador’s south shore.

programme.title: Robert
programme.desc: Robert Stephenson and his girlfriend Nancy search the Big Island of Hawaii for the perfect secluded spot to camp out. But when an unknown assailant attacks from the surrounding trees, Robert is lured to his death.

programme.title: Carol
programme.desc: Carol Schlosberg is a talented American artist enjoying a vacation with her boyfriend in Puerto Escondido, Mexico. But when she goes for a solo walk on the beach, she never returns – and the hunt is on for a killer.

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: When a well-loved married couple are found murdered in their St Maarten condo, an entire island is left in shock. But with no suspects and minimal evidence, will police be able to unravel the mystery behind the slayings before it’s too late?

programme.title: S02E07 Christine
programme.desc: Christine Barbara Rudnicki disappears while walking along Tumon Bay in Guam. Her badly beaten body is later discovered with clear signs of sexual assault. Authorities suspect there is a serial-rapist-turned-killer loose in their community.

programme.title: Tatiana & Liubov
programme.desc: Two Russian friends escape the harsh Siberian winter on a dream vacation in Thailand. But when the pair become targets of a deadly gunman, the dream turns into a nightmare.

programme.title: War Against Japan.
programme.desc: In August 1945 the Red Army launched a devastating assault against Japanese-occupied Manchuria on the same day Japan suffered its second nuclear attack, which helped force the Japanese leadership to recognise that the war was lost.

programme.title: Napper 28: Death on The Great Plains
programme.desc: A C-47 transport plane, with a crew of four, is ferrying 24 trainee P-47 fighter pilots from the Bruning Army Air Field in Nebraska to the South Dakotan Pierre Army Air Field for advanced training.

programme.title: Conspiracy Or Confusion? The Duke of Kent Crash
programme.desc: It is the 25th of August, 1942. A Short Sunderland Flying Boat slams into Eagle Rock, a remote hillside in the far north of Scotland, killing fourteen people on board. Miraculously, there is one survivor.

programme.title: Nuclear Stand Off
programme.desc: A Greenpeace ship is bombed in 1985, killing Fernando Pereira. The French authorities deny responsibility and questions over nuclear restrictions arise again.

programme.title: The Invasion of Grenada
programme.desc: In 1979, Maurice Bishop takes control of Grenada. Four years later, he is assassinated. Is this another case of the US using its might to stop Communism?

programme.title: Spy Game
programme.desc: Exploring the machines that hide in the shadows, capturing, transmitting and analysing vital combat information.

programme.title: Nuclear Near Miss
programme.desc: In 1961, Soviet T-54 tanks and American M-48 Patton tanks face off at Checkpoint Charlie in Berlin.

programme.title: Doomed Vessels
programme.desc: Explore the design flaws and human errors that condemned great ships to a tragic end. In 1628 the warship Vasa sank on her maiden voyage. Together with Vasa curator Fred Hocker, solve the mystery of why this great ship sank.

programme.title: Destroyers And Cruisers
programme.desc: Destroyers and Cruisers showcases ships that with their speed and firepower became the backbones of 20th century navies. We tell the incredible story of USS Laffey, the WW2 destroyer that would come to be known as ‘the ship that would not die’.

programme.title: The Lancaster: Britain’s Frontline Bomber.
programme.desc: The Avro Lancaster is the legendary British heavy bomber that carved its place in history with Bomber Command during World War 2. That conflict saw over seven thousand of these iconic aircraft come off the production line.

programme.title: Last Hotel on the Left
programme.desc: At the site of a famous 19th-century resort hotel, Ted has to deal with a mountain of salvage and a ghost. While Brandon and Nick struggle to remove a high-value bar from the lobby, Ted finds a curious old trunk that may be over 300 years old.

programme.title: Mini Cooper
programme.desc: The team know the BMW Mini is a popular car - but with so many of them around, will it be too difficult to make one that stands out from the crowd?

programme.title: Citroen 2CV
programme.desc: The guys are looking for any French car that is not a 2CV - but once they come across this one, it's impossible to turn down.

programme.title: Mercedes SLK
programme.desc: Since he can't get the roof to work, the owner of a Mercedes SLK decides to sell it to the team - who quickly recognise the expensive and complicated problems.

programme.title: Ford Orion
programme.desc: Everyone wants a classic Ford, so the team target an Orion. There's just one problem - the steering wheel is on the wrong side.

programme.title: Rolls Royce
programme.desc: When people talk about the quality of the classic Rolls Royce, they don't mean this one in Yorkshire. Cheap and full of mould - what's the worst that can happen?

programme.title: Audi 100
programme.desc: Gus and Will are looking for a BMW, but Gus is drawn to a classic Audi that catches his eye and manages to convince Will - who falls in love with it too.

programme.title: Breakdown At Tiffany's
programme.desc: A man brings in a 160-year-old Tiffany watch. But with no official markings on it, will Les be tempted by his $40,000 asking price?

programme.title: Gold Crash
programme.desc: Les buys memorabilia from the last remaining Bill Knapp's restaurant for $200, but Ashley fears that it won't sell

programme.title: Harold's Gamble
programme.desc: Les offers to buy a diamond-encrusted Audemars Piguet watch from a customer but asks his watch expert take a closer look at it first.

programme.title: The Outsider
programme.desc: With the price of gold continuing to drop and the store still losing money, Seth brings in a consultant to determine the best solution to the problem.

programme.title: Seth's Secret
programme.desc: Les feels hurt when he finds out that Seth is at a rival pawn shop and fears that he is quitting American Jewelry to join the competition.

programme.title: Dover
programme.desc: Dan ventures behind the battlements of Dover Castle, exploring the underground lairs and a vast network of tunnels that have been used to defend the country for centuries.

programme.title: Plantagenet Canterbury
programme.desc: Alice studies the history of Canterbury, learning the true horror of the Black Death and exploring the iconic cathedral.

programme.title: Stewart Sturling
programme.desc: Alice explores the story of Stewart Britain by visiting the home of the Scottish royals who eventually took the English throne in 1603 and created Great Britain.

programme.title: The U-Boat Terror
programme.desc: Ross completes his specialist SCUBA training under the expert tutelage of dive Supervisor Neil Brock, and inspired by the stories of his shipwrecked relatives, he heads up to the Royal Navy’s historic naval base in the far northerly Orkney Islands.

programme.title: The Gang Code
programme.desc: Experts reveal the highly encrypted phones that criminal gangs use to stay under the radar, and explain how the police are turning the phones against the crooks.

programme.title: North
programme.desc: The archaeologists get more than they bargained for while exploring the childhood home of Lady Jane Grey.

programme.title: South
programme.desc: Dig into how a lobster helped archaeologists discover an 8000-year-old underwater settlement.

programme.title: Dover
programme.desc: Dan ventures behind the battlements of Dover Castle, exploring the underground lairs and a vast network of tunnels that have been used to defend the country for centuries.

programme.title: Tower of London
programme.desc: Dan delves into the rich past of the Tower of London, exploring inside the imposing walls that have witnessed some of the most important events in our history.

programme.title: Norman Winchester
programme.desc: Alice tries some eel pie and investigates the origins of modern surgery as she explores the Norman period of British history by examining the town of Winchester.

programme.title: Gateway To Hell
programme.desc: A fire-breathing crater burns in the middle of a desert, and some suspect it is a gateway to hell. Also, a deadly shark is found lurking in a golf course water hazard. A giant white blob threatens a town in California.

programme.title: Episode 1
programme.desc: A sinister shape appears over an American city. An Australian fights for his life after being shot by an unexplained object. And a US military fighter jet appears to be being taunted by a UFO over the infamous Bermuda Triangle.

programme.title: Bullet Proof Boobs, Amazing Coincidences, Directional Cows
programme.desc: Cows align with the Earth magnetic fields, and a young makes an incredible connection.

programme.title: Weird Animal Behaviour
programme.desc: Three extraordinary stories about the supersensory power of animals: in Germany, a seemingly psychic octopus correctly predicts multiple winners in the 2010 soccer World Cup.

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: There are three types of close encounters with aliens, sightings, fleeting interactions, and the third kind where aliens take humans for extended periods. We look at instances of all three and meet real people who have experienced them.

programme.title: 1952 Wave, The
programme.desc: In Spring 1952, reports of UFO sightings shot through the roof. Can former US Air Officials shed any light on this?

programme.title: Episode 2
programme.desc: A strange structure shaped like a giant shark might point the way to a lost civilisation's Fountain of Youth. A massive hurricane heads toward Florida but something unexpected is in its eye. Bizarre marks across the eastern US leave experts baffled.

programme.title: Episode 3
programme.desc: A ghost ship with links to the mob suddenly appears on a California beach. Strange markings could be a coded message that reveals a plot to overthrow the Queen of England. A lake believed to have magical healing powers holds clues to life on Mars.

programme.desc: A fire-breathing crater burns in the middle of a desert, and some suspect it is a gateway to hell. Also, a deadly shark is found lurking in a golf course water hazard. A giant white blob threatens a town in California.

programme.title: The Old College Try

programme.title: Christmas
programme.desc: Al does his yearly pre-Christmas inspection of the family and can find they've done nothing wrong, which means he has to buy them presents, but he has to take a job as a Department Store Santa to pay for the gifts.

programme.title: Wedding Show
